content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 307 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-3662        322-5600          322-5931          322-6441          322-6444

March 29, 1982

Mr. Steven Slade
c/o William G. Peters
Supervisor - Fifth District
1200 Aguajito Road
Monterey, CA  93940

Dear Mr. Slade:

     This is to confirm our telephone conversation regarding your
reporting requirements as to your wife's business interest. You
are filing under the Conflict of Interest Code of the Monterey
Peninsula Youth Project. Your disclosure category states in
part:

     Employees in this category shall disclose all reportable
     interests in real property and income, and any business
     entity in which the person is a director, officer, partner,
     trustee, employee or holds any position of management.

     As we discussed, it is unusual that your disclosure category
does not require the reporting of investments. I feel that this
exclusion may have been an oversight and we will be contacting
the County Counsel to discuss the Code. However, your current
reporting is according to the Code as it is presently written.

     The Statement you are filing is an Assuming Office Statement.
Your wife owns a 50% partnership interest in a CPA firm located
in Monterey County. Under the terms of your disclosure category,
you must report your wife's real property interests, including
any interest held by the partnership. You would not be required
to disclose her investments.

     In subsequent years, you will also be required to report
your community property interest in your wife's pro rata share of
gross receipts from the CPA firm.

     If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
call me at 916/322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:km

State of California

Memorandum

To    : Janis                                    Date  : February 16, 19

From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Jeanette E. Turvill

Subject: Monterey Peninsula Youth Project

On February 5, Mr. Steve Slade, who was just appointed to
the Monterey Peninsula Youth Project, requested telephone
advice as to his reporting obligations of his spouse's
one-half interest in a CPA firm. He phoned the Commission
on a recommendation of the county counsel who could not
answer the question.

Mr. Slade's spouse has a one-half partnership interest in
a CPA firm located within Monterey County. The Conflict
of Interest Code for the Monterey Peninsula Youth Project
asks for reportable interests in which the person is a
director, officer, partner, trustee, employee of holds any
position of management provided that the reportable interest
is located within the jurisdiction.

Mr. Slade asks if he must report his community property
interest in the spouse's income even though she is not the
"person" as stated in the Code.

Income is not reportable on Assuming Office Statements, but
Mr. Slade would like a determination for use in the future.

A copy of the Code and a short memo from Mr. Slade is
attached.

Memorandum

DATE: 2-10-82

TO: Janette Paulett

FROM: [illegible]

SUBJECT: Re: telephone conversation of 2-9-82
included is cook you requested for determination
of "spouse" responsibility. Would also like to
have your thinking on CPP forms relationship to
"Controlled Corporations" in insurance is reports be
is income from such corporations against be - again
this is spouse partnership. Please call if any ?'s

Steve Slade

1200 Aguajito Road
Monterey, Calif. 93940

[WILLIAM G. PETE - Supervisor-Fifth District]
[408-649-6515]
[FEB 13 5 22 AM UL]
[County seal/emblem in upper right]

sections.question: Must Mr. Slade report his community property interest in his spouse's pro rata share of income from a CPA firm partnership in which his spouse holds a 50% partnership interest, given that the Conflict of Interest Code requires disclosure of business entities in which the person is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee or holds any position of management?
sections.conclusion: Your current reporting is according to the Code as it is presently written. Under the terms of your disclosure category, you must report your wife's real property interests, including any interest held by the partnership. You would not be required to disclose her investments. In subsequent years, you will also be required to report your community property interest in your wife's pro rata share of gross receipts from the CPA firm.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an employee must report his community property interest in his spouse's partnership interest in a CPA firm under a disclosure category that requires reporting business entities in which the person holds a management position, when the spouse is the actual partner and not the reporting individual.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that while investments need not be reported, Mr. Slade must report his wife's real property interests and, in subsequent annual filings, his community property interest in his wife's pro rata share of gross receipts from the CPA firm.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple internal memos/correspondence leading to the final advice letter dated March 29, 1982. The question and conclusion are clearly stated in the primary advice letter from Janis Shank McLean. Minor discrepancies in names across memos (Slade vs Blake, Turvill vs Sewill) appear to be transcription variations. Confidence reduced slightly due to internal memo format and name variations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must Mr. Slade report his community property interest in his spouse's pro rata share of income from a CPA firm partnership in which his spouse holds a 50% partnership interest, given that the Conflict of Interest Code requires disclosure of business entities in which the person is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee or holds any position of management?

CONCLUSION: Your current reporting is according to the Code as it is presently written. Under the terms of your disclosure category, you must report your wife's real property interests, including any interest held by the partnership. You would not be required to disclose her investments. In subsequent years, you will also be required to report your community property interest in your wife's pro rata share of gross receipts from the CPA firm.

embedding.summary: FPPC Counsel advises Steven Slade regarding his reporting obligations for his wife's 50% partnership interest in a CPA firm under the Monterey Peninsula Youth Project's Conflict of Interest Code. The advice confirms that while investment reporting is not required under the applicable disclosure category, real property interests and community property interests in partnership income must be disclosed in appropriate filings.
